design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed derived from title+kind+audience+generated_at -> selected architecture=comparison-lab, typography=Cabinet Grotesk, components=[gapless-bento-grid, decision-matrix, source-card-dossier], GSAP=[card-stacking, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: Attention(hero headline), Interest(comparison+bento), Desire(media+motion), Action(CTA band). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk from Fontshare verified. Hero Math Verification: H1 max-width 1120px, clamp sizing keeps 2–3 lines across breakpoints. Bento Density Verification: grid-auto-flow:dense present in CSS. GSAP Motion Selection: guarded card stacking and hover physics only. Label Sweep & Button Check: primary black pill CTA contrasts on paper background; secondary white CTA verified.
headline: Super vs Gemini: choosing a personal AI agent for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Gemini. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats.
site_brief.title: Super vs Gemini

summary: A fair, source‑backed comparison of Super and Google Gemini for people deciding how to run repeated computer‑use workflows. Gemini Spark brings powerful desktop automation to macOS. Super focuses on durable personal agents with a reusable computer‑use cache that compounds value over time.
